comments.body: How much of your code is getting called at all? If you can't tell, add some debug logging. That is, is your code not getting called - like you aren't properly registered/configured with Spring Security, or is your code getting called but not working?
code is getting called but not working i have checked in debug mode also
Add those details to the question. Plus, exactly what you do see. We can't tell what your debug shows, where things failed, if the user and password are correct, etc.

I have manually checked the username and password from the mysql workbench by sung quiery it was found but from my spring boot application it was failed to validate by checking with same username and password

answers.body:
• May be User not found in database .
    If the user isn't found, authentication will fail. Verify that your database contains the correct username/email.
    Manually check if the user exists in your database.
    SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = 'your_username' OR email = 'your_email';

  • In your AuthServiceImpl
    before authentication must ensure that user exist or not .

  • Test it on postman in correct way

    • Method: POST

    • URL: http://localhost:7878/api/auth/login

    • Headers:

      • Content-Type: application/json

    • Body (JSON):

    { "usernameOrEmail": "testuser", "password": "testpassword" }

title: How to test my Login Rest API from the Postman Client by using Spring Boot
body:
I am trying to implement the Login Rest API for my Spring boot project and It throws the Status code as 500 internal server Error in the Postman Response

This the logic implementation I have used in my controller for my login REST API:

@PostMapping("/login")
    public ResponseEntity<String> login(@RequestBody LoginDto loginDto){
        String response=authService.login(loginDto);
        return  ResponseEntity.ok(response);
    }

And this is the logic for the Service implementation to validate the given details from database:

@Service
@RequiredArgsConstructor
public class AuthServiceImpl implements AuthService{
  private final AuthenticationManager authenticationManager;

  @Override
    public String login(LoginDto loginDto) {
        Authentication authentication=authenticationManager.authenticate(new UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken(
                loginDto.getUsernameOrEmail(), loginDto.getPassword()));
        SecurityContextHolder.getContext().setAuthentication(authentication);
        
        return "User Logged-in Successfully";
    }

}

I have tried to test this API using this created url: http://localhost:7878/api/auth/login in Postman client.

The expected output is, it should show the status code- 200 and return the "User Logged-in Successfully"

but when I am trying to test this API, it gives me this type of error message in my console:

org.springframework.security.authentication.BadCredentialsException: Bad credentials

How can I resolve this issue?
